_comment: Golden queries replayed by `just rag-replay-local` (Docker OpenSearch, real hybrid kNN+keyword query) and `just rag-replay-aws` (deployed domain, real Bedrock embeddings). Each query embeds fixtures/rag/corpus.jsonl via the pinned Cohere profile at replay time (input_type=search_query) — text is stable, embeddings are computed live, so this proves the actual retrieval decision rather than pre-baked vectors. `expectedTopChunkIds` must appear within the top `topK` results; `mustNotContainChunkIds` enforces the account-isolation and negative-match assertions.

queries.queryText: When is the Meridian contract review call happening?
queries.note: Cross-channel (gmail + sms) match on the same topic; the same-topic doc in a DIFFERENT account must never appear — this is the primary account-isolation assertion.

queries.queryText: Is there an overdue invoice I need to follow up on?
queries.note: Cross-source (communication + asana) match — proves Asana context chunks retrieve alongside communications on the same topic.

queries.queryText: What time is the product launch newsletter going out?
How should client emails about billing be worded?
queries.note: Seeded org-doc source_type retrieves for a style/voice question.

queries.queryText: What days does Alex take external meetings?
queries.note: Seeded user-preference source_type retrieves for a scheduling question.
Same query scoped to acct_other must return ONLY acct_other's chunk, never acct_alex's — the permission boundary from the other direction.
Explicit project metadata filter (cross-channel linking, README L28) narrows a generic query to one workstream.

findRelatedQueries.note: findRelated({ topic }) via filterSearch (no knn clause) against real OpenSearch — cross-channel (gmail+sms) same-topic linking, cross-account isolation held even though acct_other shares the exact topic name.
findRelated({ participant }) via filterSearch against real OpenSearch — cross-channel (gmail+sms) linking on a shared participant identity, no vector similarity involved.
